deep_dive.title: LaCache: Exact Caching and Precision-Adaptive Inference for Diffusion Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion-based Large Language Models(DLLMs) enable parallel generation via Semi-Autoregressive (SAR) decoding in text generation. However, current methods suffer from severe operator-level redundancy: they recompute the entire sequence during denoising steps, ignoring that the prefix and masked suffix remain invariant within a block. We propose LaCache, a training-free acceleration framework that alleviates this redundancy through lossless caching and mixed precision. Specifically, LaCache employs Lossless State Memoization (LSM) by caching three types of intermediate results: (i) EmbedCache for embedding outputs, (ii) RoPECache for token-wise pre-attention states, and (iii) FACache for the online softmax statistics within FlashAttention. These caches allow the model to skip redundant computation on unchanged tokens without altering the output. To further alleviate memory-bandwidth bottlenecks, LaCache inegrates a per-group FP8 quantization strategy for FFN layers, tailored to step-dependent activation distributions across the diffusion process. Experiments demonstrate that LaCache alone achieves approximately 1.3X end-to-end speedup over vanilla DLLM. When combined with existing acceleration methods, LaCache reaches up to 40.2X end-to-end speedup while maintaining comparable task accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: training-free caching framework for diffusion LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 训练无关的缓存框架用于扩散大语言模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 训练无关的缓存框架用于扩散大语言模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 训练无关的缓存框架用于扩散大语言模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LaCache proposes a training-free acceleration framework for diffusion LLMs using lossless state memoization and mixed precision, significantly reducing computation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散大语言模型（DLLMs）在去噪步骤中反复计算不变前缀，存在严重冗余。本文提出训练无关的LaCache加速框架，通过无损状态记忆化缓存嵌入、RoPE和注意力统计等中间结果，并结合混合精度推理。实验结果显示，LaCache在不损失生成质量的前提下大幅减少计算量。该工作为扩散文本模型的训练无关加速提供了高效解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散大语言模型（DLLMs）在去噪步骤中反复计算不变前缀，存在严重冗余。本文提出训练无关的LaCache加速框架，通过无损状态记忆化缓存嵌入、RoPE和注意力统计等中间结果，并结合混合精度推理。实验结果显示，LaCache在不损失生成质量的前提下大幅减少计算量。该工作为扩散文本模型的训练无关加速提供了高效解决方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: text diffusion model acceleration without training

deep_dive.title: MeanFlowNFT: Bringing Forward-Process RL to Average-Velocity Generators
deep_dive.abstract: MeanFlow generators achieve fast few-step sampling by predicting average velocities over time intervals, making them attractive for efficient generation. Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a powerful way to align diffusion and flow models with human preferences and task-specific objectives. In particular, DiffusionNFT offers an efficient forward-process RL framework that does not require reverse-process trajectories or likelihood estimation. However, applying such RL methods to MeanFlow remains underexplored. DiffusionNFT optimizes instantaneous velocities, whereas MeanFlow samples with average velocities. To bridge this gap, we introduce MeanFlowNFT. Inspired by the MeanFlow identity, which bridges average and instantaneous velocities, we construct an induced instantaneous-velocity predictor. We apply the DiffusionNFT objective to this predictor, making reward optimization well-defined for MeanFlow. Sampling remains based on the average velocity, preserving MeanFlow's fast few-step generation. We further prove that MeanFlowNFT inherits DiffusionNFT's strict policy-improvement guarantee. Experiments on image and video generation show that MeanFlowNFT consistently improves baselines. Moreover, it outperforms prior state-of-the-art RL-tuned few-step generators on most metrics ($6$ of $8$ on SD3.5-M), and can even surpass multi-step RL-tuned diffusion while using only a few sampling steps. For instance, on Wan 2.1, $4$-step MeanFlowNFT reaches a VBench score of $84.33$, surpassing $50$-step LongCat-Video RL ($82.57$).
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MeanFlowNFT, bringing forward-process RL to MeanFlow generators for fast few-step sampling and alignment.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对MeanFlow生成器快速少步采样的潜力以及RL对齐的挑战，提出MeanFlowNFT，利用MeanFlow恒等式将基于前向过程的RL扩展到平均速度生成器，实现无需反向过程的高效少步采样和对齐，提升了生成速度与质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对MeanFlow生成器快速少步采样的潜力以及RL对齐的挑战，提出MeanFlowNFT，利用MeanFlow恒等式将基于前向过程的RL扩展到平均速度生成器，实现无需反向过程的高效少步采样和对齐，提升了生成速度与质量。

quick_skim.title: Efficient Difficulty-Aware Dynamic Routing for Diffusion-Based Real-World Image Super-Resolution
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based methods have achieved impressive performance in real-world image super-resolution (Real-ISR) by leveraging large pre-trained stable diffusion (SD) models as powerful generative priors. However, these methods still face two key limitations. First, existing SD-based one-step and multi-step Real-ISR approaches adopt a unified processing paradigm for all input samples, ignoring the varying restoration difficulty across images. Second, the aggressive resolution reduction of the VAE in SD models (e.g., 8x downsampling) leads to irreversible loss of fine-scale details, which cannot be recovered by the subsequent diffusion process.   To address these limitations, we propose a Difficulty-aware Dynamic Routing (DDR) strategy that overcomes the rigid, one-size-fits-all processing paradigm. Specifically, we first design a difficulty estimator to predict the restoration cost of each input image, enabling automatic assignment to a network of appropriate capacity. Then, we construct a set of Real-ISR networks with varying model capacities by modulating the spatial downsampling ratio of the VAE in the SD backbone, thereby preserving more high-frequency information for challenging cases while maintaining efficiency for simpler inputs.   Extensive experiments have demonstrated the superior efficiency and effectiveness of the proposed model compared to recent state-of-the-art methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: difficulty-aware dynamic routing for efficient super-resolution
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于困难感知的动态路由加速图像超分辨率扩散推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于困难感知的动态路由加速图像超分辨率扩散推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于困难感知的动态路由加速图像超分辨率扩散推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DDR introduces difficulty-aware dynamic routing to adaptively adjust computation per sample, accelerating diffusion-based super-resolution without sacrificing quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散超分辨率方法对所有样本采用统一处理，忽略了个体差异。DDR提出困难感知动态路由策略，根据输入难度自适应分配计算资源，避免不必要的计算浪费。实验表明该框架在保持复原质量的同时显著降低了推理时间，为实际应用提供了高效方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有扩散超分辨率方法对所有样本采用统一处理，忽略了个体差异。DDR提出困难感知动态路由策略，根据输入难度自适应分配计算资源，避免不必要的计算浪费。实验表明该框架在保持复原质量的同时显著降低了推理时间，为实际应用提供了高效方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: accelerated image generation using diffusion models

quick_skim.title: ST-Veto: Spatio-Temporal Token Veto for Diffusion MLLMs via Taylor Prediction and Visual Grounding
quick_skim.abstract: Vision Language Models (VLMs) achieve strong reasoning with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting but incur high sequential-generation cost, error accumulation, and limited self-correction. Diffusion Multimodal Large Language Models (dMLLMs) unmask tokens in an order-agnostic process, improving efficiency and enabling iterative refinement, yet their reasoning and how to enhance it remain underexplored. We propose a training-free method, Spatio-Temporal Token Veto (ST-Veto), which leverages the ability to observe all token positions at each diffusion step. Rather than relying only on current-step confidence, ST-Veto vetoes temporally unstable tokens via second-order Taylor prediction of confidence dynamics and filters weakly grounded tokens using image-attention mass, swapping them with safer candidates. Across multiple dMLLMs and multimodal reasoning benchmarks, ST-Veto consistently outperforms standard decoding policies and prior VLM reasoning methods, improving accuracy by up to 9% with no additional training or generation cost. Analyses show that ST-Veto steers generation toward higher-confidence, better-grounded paths.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training-free token veto method for diffusion MLLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 训练无关的令牌否决方法用于扩散多模态大模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 训练无关的令牌否决方法用于扩散多模态大模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 训练无关的令牌否决方法用于扩散多模态大模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ST-Veto proposes a training-free spatio-temporal token veto method using Taylor prediction and visual grounding to improve efficiency and reasoning in diffusion MLLMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散多模态大模型（dMLLMs）推理效率低和视觉定位弱的问题，本文提出训练无关的时空令牌否决（ST-Veto）方法。该方法利用二阶泰勒预测令牌的稳定性，否决不稳定令牌，并结合视觉定位过滤弱定位令牌。实验表明，ST-Veto在多个基准上显著提升了生成质量并减少了推理开销。这项工作为训练无关的扩散模型加速提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对扩散多模态大模型（dMLLMs）推理效率低和视觉定位弱的问题，本文提出训练无关的时空令牌否决（ST-Veto）方法。该方法利用二阶泰勒预测令牌的稳定性，否决不稳定令牌，并结合视觉定位过滤弱定位令牌。实验表明，ST-Veto在多个基准上显著提升了生成质量并减少了推理开销。这项工作为训练无关的扩散模型加速提供了新思路。
